description: Following President Lula da Silva's inauguration in January 2023, Brazil engaged in a high-stakes battle to reactivate the Amazon Fund, an international mechanism to finance anti-deforestation efforts. The scenario tracks public support for state-led environmental protection versus the economic interests of the agribusiness sector in the Brazilian Amazon.

key_events.description: President Lula officially reactivates the Amazon Fund via decree on January 1, 2023.
Germany and Norway announce the resumption of financial contributions to the fund.
High-profile anti-deforestation raids by IBAMA in the Yanomami territory receive broad media coverage.
Government reports a 33% decline in Amazon deforestation rates during the first half of the year.

calibration_notes: This scenario demonstrates the volatility of environmental policy in emerging economies where economic development (agribusiness) and global ecological preservation agendas are in direct conflict.
